matchValues: No payment methods are available for this order. Please review your details or contact us for help.
Enter your billing details to view available payment methods.
No payment is needed for this order.
Because your browser does not support JavaScript, totals may update only after you confirm with the %s button.
entries.rationale: WooCommerce checkout compatibility still requires local helper copy around payment availability, no-payment handling, and noscript flow. This debt is documented and intentionally kept out of delta noise until checkout compatibility consolidation.
These are shell-level labels around an already governed checkout flow. They remain temporarily local so the phase can stabilize baseline behavior before label normalization work.
Runtime fallback wording still exists in the loading bridge even though SSR data attributes are present. Keep it reviewable as temporary migration debt until runtime fallback removal is scheduled.

matchValues: Please review the highlighted checkout details.
entries.rationale: Checkout runtime currently carries mirrored fallback copy for validation and loading. This is known baseline debt and should not drown out future governance regressions.

matchValues: Enter your apartment, suite, or unit if needed.
Confirm the required checkbox to continue.
entries.rationale: Checkout field-level validation remains runtime-local for now. This is documented runtime governance debt and should only resurface when it changes or escalates.

matchValues: Shipping and taxes calculated at checkout.
entries.rationale: Cart summary helper copy is known trust-adjacent debt outside the governed cart drawer family. It remains visible as baseline debt but should not be reported as a fresh regression every scan.

matchValues: We could not update the bag just now. Please try again.
entries.rationale: Drawer runtime is already registry-driven, but SSR still carries duplicate fallback literals for resilience. Keep this documented until the surface family completes fallback deduplication.

matchValues: We could not update the bag just now. Please try again.
entries.rationale: The cart helper layer still mirrors governed drawer strings locally as a resilience fallback. This is tracked duplicate-fallback debt and should stay quiet until fallback cleanup begins.

matchValues: We could not update the bag just now. Please try again.
entries.rationale: Cart drawer runtime still keeps a local fallback for error recovery even though SSR data attributes provide the governed string. This is temporary migration debt and must stay removable.
Secondary runtime cart messages still exist inline even after the governed error path landed. They are documented migration debt and should not appear as fresh delta on every run.

matchValues: We could not update the bag just now. Please try again.
entries.rationale: Add-to-cart runtime shares the same inline fallback as the cart drawer flow. It is a known baseline debt and should collapse into the governed runtime path later.

matchValues: Select an option before adding to bag.
entries.rationale: Variation-selection feedback is still runtime-local while the rest of the cart feedback family moves through governed SSR bridges. Keep it visible as deferred migration debt.

matchValues: A secure password will be prepared and sent after registration.
entries.rationale: WooCommerce account registration copy still falls back locally in the auth template. This is unresolved legacy surface debt and remains deferred until account auth consolidation.

matchValues: Use the details below for a smoother return to checkout next time.
entries.rationale: The address form still includes a local fallback even though the governed account surface exists. This is accepted as temporary migration debt, not as silent bypass.

matchValues: Keep billing and shipping destinations ready for a faster checkout.
entries.rationale: Account helper fallbacks still mix structural and trust-adjacent language inline. Keep them explicitly documented so later account consolidation can remove them without ambiguity.

matchValues: Manage billing and shipping destinations with spacious, touch-safe forms and restrained utility language.
Update your name, email, or password in a single uninterrupted form surface.
Restore access with the same clear rhythm as the rest of the customer account experience.
Review recent purchases, delivery progress, and order details.
Update name, email, and password in one simple account form.
entries.rationale: Account helper prose still contains known legacy copy outside the final registry path. It is stable enough for baseline treatment but remains scheduled debt.
Recovery controls remain inline for compatibility with WooCommerce account templates. They are documented structural exceptions and must be revisited during account label normalization.
Order-detail fallbacks are already documented deferred debt. They stay in the baseline until the next account consolidation pass rather than reappearing as fresh governance noise.
Password form labels are structural account controls, not tonal helper copy. They are accepted into the baseline as reviewable label exceptions until a dedicated normalization phase exists.
Auth-shell labels and recovery links remain inline by design in this phase. They are documented exceptions, not hidden bypasses.
Address-form headings are low-risk structural labels. Keep them baseline-documented until account label normalization is explicitly scheduled.
Reset-password heading is a structural action label. It stays reviewable in the baseline rather than being silently ignored.

matchValues: Only customers who have purchased this item can leave a review, which keeps the conversation grounded in ownership.
entries.rationale: Review access messaging is a known deferred surface outside the current trust-critical closure path. It remains reviewable debt, not a hidden exclusion.

matchValues: Measured notes from customers, arranged with the same quiet hierarchy as the rest of the product story.
entries.rationale: Review-experience helper copy is outside the current governance closure. It is deferred baseline debt, not an untracked leak.
Search retry wording is a small local interaction label. It is documented as deferred discovery debt rather than treated as a fresh regression.
Search trigger labels are structural discovery controls. They are reviewable exceptions until label normalization broadens into search IA work.
